capabilities: Combine fuzz testing with agent-based planning to identify security vulnerabilities by simulating intelligent attack strategies.

procedure.action: Instrument the codebase with a coverage tool like LLVM's libFuzzer to track execution paths during fuzz testing using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Develop agent-based models using a framework like OpenAI Gym to simulate intelligent attack strategies using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Run fuzz tests with the agent models, adjusting parameters to maximize code coverage and identify vulnerabilities using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Query and analyze coverage data to identify untested paths and potential security weaknesses by querying each source in parallel with timeout_ms < 5000; deduplicate results on record_id; assert collection_success_rate >= 0.95.

Run on agent strategies and fuzzing parameters based on coverage data to improve test effectiveness up to max_iterations=10; halt when convergence_delta < 0.01 or no further improvement detected; log iteration_count and final_metric; assert final_metric > initial_metric.

summary: Integrate coverage-guided fuzz testing with agent planning to enhance security testing. Define success metrics, validation steps, and criteria for evaluating the effectiveness of the testing process.
title: Coverage-Guided Fuzz Testing with Agent Planning

failureConditions: Insufficient code coverage leading to missed vulnerabilities.

Agent models fail to simulate realistic attack scenarios; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Fuzzing parameters are not optimized, resulting in ineffective testing; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
successConditions: Verify that code coverage exceeds 80% across critical components
Ensure that agent models generate diverse and realistic attack scenarios
Confirm that identified vulnerabilities are reproducible and valid
